title: Țara surâsului prezidențial
summary: The article criticizes Romanian President Nicușor Dan for his lack of foreign policy expertise, his absence from the Davos Economic Forum, and his ambiguous positioning between the US and EU on issues like Greenland and trade tariffs. Author Rodica Culcer argues that Dan's presidential advisor Marius Lazurcă attempts to justify contradictory diplomatic positions, particularly the claim that Romania is 'solidary with both antagonistic positions' of the US and EU.
talking_points: Nicușor Dan's absence from Davos is symptomatic of Romania's diplomatic irrelevance, not a strategic choice
Presidential advisor Lazurcă's claim that Romania is 'solidary with both antagonistic positions' of the US and EU is intellectually incoherent
Dan's Facebook post on the Greenland crisis signals a distancing from the EU and alignment with Washington, as identified by Oxford professor Corneliu Bjola

The president fails to defend the rule of law domestically, with unresolved issues around the CCR blockage on magistrates' special pensions and the aftermath of Russian interference in the 2024 elections
Dan appears to be manipulated by shadowy figures like Marius Lazurcă, who has shown anti-Ukrainian sympathies
author: Rodica Culcer
